title: TVA to Buy Energy from Oak Ridge Advanced Nuclear Plant to Power Google Data Centers
content: Google, Kairos Power, and the Tennessee Valley Authority (TVA) announced a landmark deal in which TVA will purchase 50 MW of clean, around-the-clock electricity from Kairos Powerâ€™s Hermes 2 advanced nuclear plant in Oak Ridge, Tenn., to power Google data centers in Tennessee and Alabama starting in 2030. The project, part of Google and Kairosâ€™s broader plan to bring 500 MW of next-generation nuclear online by 2035, marks the first U.S. utility PPA for a GEN IV reactor and will see Hermes 2â€™s capacity expanded from 28 MW to 50 MW. Through the TVA system, Google will secure the carbon-free energy attributes to decarbonize operations while supporting regional growth, as TVA and Kairos highlight the agreement as a model for scaling affordable, firm nuclear power to meet rising digital economy demand and enhance U.S. energy security.
author: Analysts Account
